steps.title: Use the square fact
steps.content: For any real value of $x$, the square $(x-1)^2$ cannot be negative. Its smallest possible value is $0$.

steps.title: Compare both sides
steps.content: The equation requires that square to equal $-4$, which is impossible for real numbers. On a graph, the curves do not intersect.

steps.title: Count the real solutions
steps.content: Since no real square can equal $-4$, the equation has zero distinct real solutions. The answer is D.
